expectations.text: Explains beats and segments parameters of Euclidean notation
expectations.evidence: "First number (3) = number of beats/hits to place" and "Second number (8) = total number of steps in the sequence"

expectations.text: Mentions Euclidean rhythm or distributing-evenly concept
expectations.evidence: "Euclidean rhythms — a concept in algorithmic music where you distribute a number of beats as evenly as possible across a total number of time steps"

expectations.text: Mentions the optional third offset parameter
expectations.evidence: "There may be additional parameters such as an offset or rotation value (a third number), but I would recommend checking the official Strudel documentation" — mentioned vaguely without a concrete example like (3,8,2) and with uncertainty

expectations.text: Provides at least one code example with Euclidean notation
expectations.evidence: Code block with s("bd(3,8)"), s("hh(5,8)"), s("sd(2,8)"), and s("bd(3,8), hh(5,8)")
